name: Cashville Gold & Silver Buyers, Inc.
description_short: Family-owned precious metals buyer in Nashville specializing in gold, silver, jewelry, coins, and diamonds with 55+ years of experience.
description_long:
Cashville Gold & Silver Buyers, Inc. is a Nashville-based precious metals purchasing business that has operated for over 55 years. The company is a third-generation, father-and-son-owned local enterprise founded and led by CEO Jerry Levine. According to their website, they have been voting as the number-one place to sell gold and silver in Tennessee for 17 consecutive years and have received recognition from local media including News Channel 5 TV and Fox 17.

The company buys a wide range of precious metal items including broken or unwanted gold and silver jewelry, rare coins, bullion (gold, silver, platinum, palladium), diamonds, luxury watches (Rolex, Tag Heuer, Tiffany pieces), flatware, candlesticks, and estate jewelry collections. They explicitly state they do not require customers to clean or polish items before selling, as they purchase items specifically for their metal and gemstone content for recycling purposes. Transactions occur in person at their Nashville location (2500 8th Ave S, Nashville TN 37204).

Cashville Gold distinguishes itself through longevity and local community presence. They emphasize their decades of operational history, family ownership, neighborhood familiarity, and on-the-spot cash payments. Their marketing strategy centers on the promise to "pay the most" and the reliability of a established local business that customers can return to repeatedly. They position themselves as trustworthy neighbors rather than transient buyers.

As a precious metals buyer rather than a lender, Cashville Gold operates in the pawn/collateral space but does not offer loans—they purchase items outright for cash. Customers should understand this is a one-time transaction business, not a credit or lending service. The company's valuations and cash offers are determined by current precious metal market prices and the company's internal assessment, with no third-party pricing transparency mentioned on their site.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: 55+ years of operational history in Nashville provides stability and established reputation
Family-owned and locally operated by third generation (Jerry Levine, CEO), creating community accountability
Voted number one for 17 consecutive years by local customers and media recognition from Fox 17 and News Channel 5
Accepts items in any condition (broken, bent, tarnished, unwanted) without requiring cleaning or preparation
Handles diverse precious metal categories including gold, silver, platinum, palladium, diamonds, coins, and watches in one location
Offers same-location, in-person transactions with face-to-face negotiations across a desk
Claims to pay competitive rates (advertises 'we pay the most') with immediate cash payment
cons: No transparent pricing methodology or market-rate guarantees published—valuations entirely at company discretion
No mention of independent appraisals, grading certifications, or third-party verification of metal content or gemstone quality
Single physical location in Nashville (2500 8th Ave S) limits access for non-local customers; no online buying or mail-in services mentioned
Website lacks specific details on fees, deductions, or how final offers are calculated from initial assessments
No information about authentication process for luxury watches or certification standards for diamonds beyond subjective claim of buying 'all styles'
best_for: Nashville-area residents with unwanted or broken gold, silver, or jewelry who want immediate local cash sales
Estate liquidators and individuals clearing deceased family member's jewelry and precious metal collections
Owners of luxury watches (Rolex, Tag Heuer) and designer jewelry (Tiffany) seeking local buyers with established reputation
diagnosis: Cashville Gold is best for Nashville residents with precious metals, jewelry, or coins they want to convert to immediate cash through an established local buyer. The main caveat is that this is a one-time purchase transaction, not a loan or credit product, and pricing is entirely at the company's discretion with no published valuation standards or third-party verification mentioned—customers should comparison shop or obtain independent appraisals before accepting offers.
services: Purchase of broken, bent, or twisted gold jewelry
Purchase of unwanted or out-of-style gold jewelry
Gold and silver coins (old, collector, rare) buying
Platinum and palladium precious metals buying
Diamond purchase (all styles, designs, sizes, and shapes)
Luxury watch buying (Rolex, Tag Heuer, other brands)
Silver flatware and silverware buying (knives, forks, spoons, serving pieces)
Estate liquidation services (comprehensive jewelry, coins, bullion, and precious metals)
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
